textByHashes.b0b8180b57755c71bc2c38afe15551a8dcdc28f6: Hello there Zanik! How can I help?
textByHashes.8f313b2d1d0e39668b758fd99b4206f161b09ebd: This is my spinning wheel! Look at the way it goes round and round and round...
textByHashes.13a8396bfc25cbcbbc44b5164d80516b636682cb: It spins wool into balls so we can work it more easily.
textByHashes.52f257d532e5df2af45ab3a3197a96ff0a6f640b: Hello there! Are you interested in hearing all about crafting?
textByHashes.13f5cc63113c3dc940f8b2c8c7ff2eb688c3a9d4: To get started, you might like to try your hand at crafting some armour from cow's leather. There's a tanner just over in Al Kharid who could tan any hides for you.
textByHashes.aec0fbfe8745003abd83cd93e404f27d4d8ed6b8: Once you've managed to get your hand on some kind of leather, you just need a needle and some thread to get to work!
textByHashes.09b48fc927ff544854b187e44f598188fbc6f473: If that doesn't take your fancy, you could try your hand at spinning wool right here on the spinning wheel.
textByHashes.e6d94153dc4f269a745145b76ea4f0f3b3d97542: Come to think of it... Farmer Fred, just north of here, sounds like he could use a hand shearing some of his sheep. Maybe you could help him out, and get some practice while you're at it.
textByHashes.3ed3e7d8725252b188a601794ab5f2a9ce60b304: Is there anything else you want to know?
textByHashes.90e2afd954de82944d12dd813d04bc46867b45ae: Lots of budding craftsmen seem to spend their time here spinning Flax into bow strings on the wheel, if that takes your fancy.
textByHashes.3e02a542f8bc93a4003efb2b1ad80f2bd564037b: Or you might like to try your hand at crafting some armour from cow's leather. There's a tanner just over in Al Kharid who could tan any hides for you.
textByHashes.4055945748d1ba85acb9de95bf487e463fc444a5: Now that you've got a little experience under your belt, the world's your oyster!
textByHashes.c5ae20d60d1ea1346a2fb1a19c847b25b4e85b94: I can't help but feel like I should be the one asking you! At this point, the world's your oyster!
textByHashes.a4cd66933965e35f04a24448ec105cb3ddd5faef: If you wanted to try your hand at making some jewellery, you'll want to get your hand on some gold and some kind of gem, with a mould to work with and a chisel at hand.
textByHashes.689473324c2f7b9d615fb5b0154e5070ebad3dd4: But if that's not your thing, maybe you'd like to try making some more armour!
textByHashes.993d9d0bd8c4795640de218e98bd9aa3497c605c: Cow's leather's a good place to start, but you can try all other kinds of hides, like a dragon's!
textByHashes.f3a6da94a66f1761cca28444ab0a29c4db44f78e: Glass blowing seems to be a popular trade, too. You can forge molten glass from some sand and soda ash, then make all kinds of things using a glass blowing pipe.
textByHashes.e395d9a4887584dd7584361768e65b0057a0cf17: Is there anything else you want to know
textByHashes.ccb36fbbc163845cc042599f61fad1823b9edfbc: All kinds of things, really! You can make armour from leather, make some pottery, try your hand at glass blowing, make some jewellery, weapons even...
textByHashes.aeab6de70fd6eccec976de5ecbabcf2b07039664: What would you like to hear about?
textByHashes.efd655e82c83e2075c9890f837159d177b016e72: Sure thing! Most armour you can craft just involves taking a needle and some thread to whatever material you can get your hands on.
textByHashes.6897faef3c9f3f30559fd32f44275a6f16fb1bdf: You might like to try with different kinds of animal hide, from cows, yaks, snakes, dragons... Or any other sort of fabric you can find!
textByHashes.4254e64d07b7f7b1d0d614d0f6d63dc55de11387: Some crafty types have started making some leather- covered wooden shields recently, too!
textByHashes.b0401779b1bad1a6a969e7f1db02f9382697d905: You might find some creatures make for a pretty sturdy helmet, too. The best way to make something like that's just to take a good old chisel to it!
textByHashes.561b6f38f2d0363de411c0c2e6ce5a3b5ace9530: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?
textByHashes.1d111b7c80d6e039e95f89d987752d5ddd6f462b: Of course! Pottery's all kind of the same, it just takes a lot of getting used to.
textByHashes.eecf1347291c194d4590f43dff0bf686707fabe1: All that there really is to it is getting your hands on some clay, getting it wet and getting to work on a potter's wheel.
textByHashes.a3ed712fd5d9f4123e56aa47e624560eb0997808: Once you've got the shape you want out of it, you want to put it in a pottery oven to fire it up.
textByHashes.1acb15ca6f79665ab8a4b9196e599a65946bb1b0: The barbarians west of Varrock seem to be pretty keen on their pottery. I'd take a look around there, if you wanted to try it out!
textByHashes.73ba12f35a7ee5fd6021546db1e48fb849c91bd1: Once you get the hang of it, you can make pots, pie dishes, bowls... You know.
textByHashes.67a10863ee04bd14fbf94971a85728d81b90a12f: Glass blowing? Well, to get started you'll need to get yourself some molten glass. You can make some for yourself by heating sand and soda ash in a furnace.
textByHashes.a936e2f4218105a56939432f5c4765875b56d4ab: Once you've got a hold of that, you'll want a glass blowing pipe to blow it out in to shape, before it cools down.
textByHashes.8d686d17092671c6221823da4627ff1b3e37edd1: It's not the easiest thing to get to grips with, but it shouldn't be too difficult!
textByHashes.934ab61247d079903aa9416a7c9ddf6ce95e54f9: Sure. All it boils down to is a bar of gold or silver, some kind of cut gem if you're feeling fancy, and a mould to help shape the thing.
textByHashes.5ae6f1447af3d29e92bcf3270558273bb8a78929: You can use a chisel to make a gem really shine and sparkle. You wouldn't want to use an uncut one for any jewellery.
textByHashes.36e6d07cb795675599c375bb6b1e3c134aa6862d: Once you've got all of your materials together, you'll want to use a furnace to actually craft whatever it is you're trying to make!
textByHashes.d8ce2fc95ba2fc7f943964e2677dd816dc03ed23: Wizards seem to like enchanting their jewellery to do all kinds of things, too, but I don't know a lot about that.
textByHashes.a287dce1c1bd5603539ad0c62ebb39175937ba0f: Well, there aren't too many weapons that people tend to craft, but there are a couple worth noting!
textByHashes.80eea5294396650cc39a989c465206ba3b08826d: Battlestaves are probably the most common. It boils down to fastening an orb of some kind onto the end of a battlestaff.
textByHashes.0ea5871f2279251b22de52fbfb8f9da967c9b799: You do need to get the orb in the first place, though... You can make one through glass blowing, but it's not much use without being charged.
textByHashes.327297d9b96b86bec1c8f65f1ffaf0fce4826b40: I can't help much with crafting it, though! There's a little more magic involved there than I'm familiar with.
textByHashes.204a8b87eeb61bb229e62100d4096300b9ebbb79: Aside from battlestaves, there are a couple of weapons that people like to make from silver... Sickles and bolts, usually.
textByHashes.61498d2633117f8e6439e184177b8bb37d4f38a1: Well, just come back any time you want to know anything!
